'''Matthew Frawley''' (born 24 December 1994) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|SO|SH}} or {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/canberra-raiders/matt-frawley/|title=Matt Frawley|website=Canberra Raiders|access-date=22 September 2020|archive-date=25 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200925041112/https://www.raiders.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/canberra-raiders/matt-frawley/|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2019/12/10/matt-frawley-returns-to-raiders/|title=Matt Frawley returns to Raiders|website=Canberra Raiders|date=10 December 2019}}</ref>

Frawley previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the NRL and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Frawley was born in [[Canberra]], [[Australian Capital Territory|ACT]], Australia. Frawley is of Irish heritage.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://twitter.com/RaidersCanberra/status/1506850096507666433/|title=Raiders Backgrounds|website=Twitter|date=24 March 2022}}</ref>

Frawley played his junior rugby league for the Belconnen United Sharks and [[West Belconnen Warriors]],<ref name="canberratimes1">{{cite web|author=Rugby League |url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/canberra-raiders-under20s-captain-matt-frawley-signs-twoyear-deal-with-the-canterbury-bulldogs-20141108-11j15i.html |title=Canberra Raiders under-20s captain Matt Frawley signs two-year deal with the Canterbury Bulldogs |publisher=Canberratimes.com.au |date=2014-11-08 |accessdate=2017-03-30}}</ref> before being signed by the [[Canberra Raiders]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2013 and 2014, Frawley played for the Canberra Raiders' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/f/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304035513/http://nycdatabase.org/players/f/ |url-status=usurped |archive-date=4 March 2016 |title=F |work=NYC DATABASE |date= 27 December 2012|accessdate=2017-03-30}}</ref> captaining the side in 2014.<ref name="canberratimes1"/> Frawley had doubts about ever making the [[NRL]] as Canberra signed [[Blake Austin]].

Frawley started selling retail clothes in Canberra before signing a 2-year contract with the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] that began in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |author=Zero Tackle |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/frawley-pens-two-year-deal-with-bulldogs-15604/ |title=Frawley pens two-year deal with Bulldogs |work=Zero Tackle |date=2014-11-05 |accessdate=2017-03-30 |archive-date=30 March 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170330181125/https://www.zerotackle.com/frawley-pens-two-year-deal-with-bulldogs-15604/ |url-status=dead }}</ref>
[[File:Matt Frawley.jpg|300px|thumb|Frawley playing for the Canterbury Bulldogs in 2016]]

===2017===
In round 5 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Frawley got the call up from coach [[Des Hasler]] and made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Canterbury against the [[Brisbane Broncos]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-bulldogs-v-broncos/tabid/10874/newsid/105196/default.aspx |title=Updated team lists: Bulldogs v Broncos |publisher=NRL.com |date=2016-12-18 |accessdate=2017-03-30}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |author=NRL Digital Media |url=http://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2017/03/30/crownbet_late_mail_r.af_news.html |title=CrownBet Late Mail: Round 5 v Broncos |publisher=Bulldogs |date= |accessdate=2017-03-30 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170330181345/http://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2017/03/30/crownbet_late_mail_r.af_news.html |archive-date=30 March 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref> due to incumbent half [[Moses Mbye]] being suspended for one-match. Frawley was named in the number 6 jersey.

In his second game in the [[NRL]], he was originally named in the number 20 jersey, coach Des Hasler moved him onto the bench in place of [[Raymond Faitala-Mariner]]. He came on late in the first half in place of injured [[Brad Abbey]], and in the first set of the second half scored a try, his first in the [[NRL]]. He also came up with a try assist, with a cut out pass to send [[Josh Morris (rugby league)|Josh Morris]] over in the corner.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/bulldogs-come-back-to-beat-knights/tabid/10874/newsid/105454/default.aspx |title=Bulldogs come back to beat Knights |publisher=NRL.com |date=2016-12-18 |accessdate=2017-04-07}}</ref>

In round 6 against [[Newcastle Knights]], Frawley was named in the number 20 jersey, he was later named on the bench in place of Raymond Faitala-Mariner. Frawley didn't get on the field in the first half, though when Brad Abbey fell down with injury, Frawley came on to the field in the second half. He scored a try. He dummied multiple times and put the Bulldogs in the lead. The Bulldogs went on to win 22-12.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

In round 9, Frawley played his former side, Canberra with Canterbury winning the game 16-10. He had to take control as [[Josh Reynolds (Australian rugby league player)|Josh Reynolds]] got injured, and was ruled out until round 14.

Frawley scored his second career try in Round 11 against the [[Sydney Roosters]], almost completing his team's comeback.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===2018===
Frawley started the 2018 season as Canterbury's first choice {{rlp|fe}}, but was demoted to reserve grade by coach [[Dean Pay]] after 2 games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/bulldogs-coach-dean-pay-reacts-to-poor-start-to-season-drops-fiveeighth-matt-frawley/news-story/abb6675c4733a7472cd718a5b57587ba|title=Bulldogs NRL team news: Matt Frawley dropped, Jeremy Marshall-King comes in|date=20 March 2018|publisher=|accessdate=8 June 2018}}</ref> After spending 7 weeks in reserve grade, Frawley was recalled to Canterbury's NRL side for their round 9 game against Brisbane.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2018/round-9/brisbane-vs-canterbury/summary.html|title=NRL 2018 - Round 9 - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|accessdate=8 June 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/broncos/broncos-v-bulldogs-team-lists-for-round-9-clash/news-story/fc0b47837048f59635d2b156794c37ec|title=Team lists: Mass changes for Canterbury|newspaper=News.com.au|date=May 2018|publisher=|accessdate=8 June 2018}}</ref>  On 27 August, Frawley was one of the players announced to be departing the club at the end of the season after his contract was not renewed.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2018/08/27/join-us-on-sunday-to-say-farewell-to-our-departing-players/|title = Join us on Sunday to say farewell to our departing players|date = 27 August 2018}}</ref>
It was announced on 28 October 2018 that Frawley had signed for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the [[Super League]].

===2019===
On 10 December, it was announced that Frawley had signed a one-year development contract with [[NRL]] side [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] for the [[2020 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2019/12/10/matt-frawley-returns-to-raiders/|title=Matt Frawley returns to Raiders|website=Raiders.com.au|date=10 December 2019}}</ref>

===2020===
In round 20 of the [[2020 NRL season]], Frawley made and scored a try on his debut for the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the role of five-eighth, against [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla-Sutherland]] in their final game of the 2020 regular season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2020/09/22/must-watch-stuart-announces-four-players-to-make-raiders-debut/|title = Must watch: Stuart announces four players to make Raiders debut| date=22 September 2020 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-09-26/nrl-parramatta-beats-wests-tigers-as-panthers-raiders-post-wins/12707470/|title=Parramatta beats Wests Tigers to claim third spot on NRL ladder, Panthers and Raiders score wins|newspaper=ABC News|date=26 September 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
Frawley made only five appearances for Canberra in the [[2021 NRL season]] which saw the club finish a disappointing 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-canberra-raiders-vs-sydney-roosters-live-blog-live-stream-supercoach-scores-videos-jack-wighton-james-tedesco/news-story/745b280dcc746da257fecd8fda231afd/|title=Roosters' 'Mr Fix It' stuns with hat-trick, Sloppy Raiders' final fade out ends season: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=2 September 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2021-roosters-v-canberra-adam-keighran-stars-as-raiders-season-ends-with-shocking-defeat/news-story/f9ef95652ed66e97e4e047245f11da7b/|title=Tough conversations coming for senior Canberra stars as interest grows in Parramatta young gun Dylan Brown|website=www.theaustralian.com.au|date=3 September 2021 }}</ref>

===2022===
Frawley made eight appearances for Canberra in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 8th on the table and qualified for the finals.  Frawley did not play in either of Canberra's finals matches.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-live-parramatta-eels-vs-canberra-raiders-teams-live-scores-brad-arthur-vs-ricky-stuart-highlights-live-stream-joseph-tapine-mitchell-moses-concussion/news-story/ab4febda34065392fcd3071590abc0ec/|title=Eels send ruthless title message, bury finals 'ghosts' in Raiders no-show: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=16 September 2022 }}</ref>

=== 2023 ===
Frawley kicked his first two-point field goal (a field goal kicked from outside the opposition's 40-metre zone) in the round 4 match against [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]]. Frawley played a total of eight games for Canberra in the [[2023 NRL season]] including the clubs elimination finals loss to Newcastle. On 10 September, it was announced that Frawley would be joining English side [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] on a two-year deal starting in 2024.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/01/01/2023-nrl-signings-tracker-latest-signings-news-details-contracts-signings-updates/|title=2023 NRL Signings Tracker: Alamoti, Schneider join Penrith|website=www.nrl.com|date=24 December 2023 }}</ref>

===2024===
Frawley played 25 games for Leeds in the 2024 Super League which saw the club finish 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk|date=9 December 2024 }}</ref>

===2025===
On 18 June 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] on one-month loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=18 Jun 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Huddersfield Giants tie down off-contract star as Matt Frawley makes loan switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-tie-down-off-contract-star-as-matt-frawley-makes-loan-switch}}</ref> On 12 September, Frawley announced his retirement .<ref>{{Cite web |last=Henshaw |first=Callum |date=2025-09-12 |title=Matt Frawley Calls Time on Rugby League Career at 30 |url=https://rugbyleaguezone.com/matt-frawley-calls-time-on-rugby-league-career-at-30-416919/ |access-date=2025-09-12 |website=Rugby League Zone |language=en-AU}}</ref>
